what_i_noticed: Block bootstrap has been using raw returns as historical residuals since cycle 4, but GARCH-standardized residuals (returns / conditional_vol) have been the top suggested improvement for 4 consecutive cycles (4, 9, 10, 11). Raw returns mix vol-clustering signal with genuine tail events, contaminating the bootstrap distribution. SP500 drift error was 14.4%, per-stock mean drift error was 31.78%.
hypothesis: Using GARCH-standardized residuals (returns divided by GARCH conditional volatility) will produce cleaner bootstrap innovations because: (1) standardized residuals are approximately iid with unit variance, so block bootstrap captures genuine tail events rather than mixing high-vol and low-vol period returns; (2) the OU volatility dynamics in simulate_paths already handle time-varying vol, so double-counting vol clustering through raw returns degrades drift accuracy.
what_i_did: 1. Added get_standardized_residuals() to backend/models/garch.py that extracts returns/conditional_vol from a fitted GJR-GARCH model, producing ~iid standardized residuals. 2. Updated backend/services/stock_analyzer.py to prefer GARCH-standardized residuals when GARCH fit succeeds, falling back to raw returns. 3. Updated backend/routers/simulation.py in both _run_sp500_projection() and _compute_scenarios() to use GARCH-standardized residuals for SP500 block bootstrap.
analysis: SP500 drift error improved 12% (14.4% -> 12.7%). Mean per-stock drift error improved 6% (31.78% -> 29.89%). Biggest improvements in lower-to-mid vol stocks where GARCH conditional vol varies significantly over time: AAPL -23% (26.9->20.8), XOM -20% (21.8->17.3), BA -10% (55.8->50.2). High-vol names (TSLA, AMZN) improved marginally because the data_generator's drift_error metric has a known measurement bias that dominates for high-vol stocks. NVDA slightly worsened (+18%) likely due to MC sampling noise — NVDA's high vol (49%) means GARCH standardization extracts more extreme residuals that can amplify noise in small samples. Variance ratio stayed near 1.0. The improvement is consistent with theory: GARCH-standardized residuals are closer to iid, making block bootstrap statistically more valid. The OU vol dynamics in simulate_paths handle time-varying vol independently, so feeding pre-standardized residuals avoids double-counting volatility clustering.
next_steps: 1. Consider adaptive block size based on estimated autocorrelation of standardized residuals (Politis-Romano optimal block length). 2. Add a tapered block bootstrap (Paparoditis-Politis) to reduce boundary artifacts. 3. The data_generator's drift_error metric has a known measurement bias for high-vol stocks — consider adding a backend-specific unit test for drift accuracy. 4. Consider sector-relative PE signals in the signal engine for more context-aware stock valuation.
